name: Cancel Payout
summary: Cancels a previously requested payout (fund withdrawal) for the given account. A POST call is made to /CancelPayout with a jData JSON object identifying the user, account and the transaction reference number of the payout to cancel, plus the jKey session token.

request_params.name: jData
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list.

json_fields.name: uid
json_fields.description: User id of the logged in user.

json_fields.name: trans_ref_num
json_fields.description: transaction reference number (number which defines each transaction)

response_fields.name: actid
response_fields.description: This will be present only in a successful response.

response_fields.name: tran_status
response_fields.description: This is used to indicate the status of transaction

response_fields.name: request_time
response_fields.description: This will be present only in a successful response.

issues: The section straddles two PDF pages: the title, intro URL, and the jData/jKey parameter table are on page 67; the jData JSON-fields table and the RESPONSE DETAILS table are on page 68. The right-column code samples are interleaved across both pages (curl + start of success response on p67; tail of success response + failure response on p68). All jData body fields and all four response fields have EMPTY 'Possible value' cells in the doc tables (verified on the page-68 image) — in particular tran_status has no enum (the success sample's "tran_status":"88" is a raw numeric status code). The sample curl uses placeholder host 'https://BaseURL/' rather than the documented piconnect host, sends trans_ref_num as an empty string, and the failure response references a different transaction ref (20211300000033) than the documented success/example refs.
notes:
- Mandatory fields (marked with * in the doc): actid, uid, trans_ref_num. brkname is optional (no asterisk).
- The jData object is sent URL/form style as jData={...}&jKey=... in the request body, matching the curl example; the Content-Type header in the sample is application/json.
- Replace 'BaseURL' in the sample curl with the real host https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI when calling /CancelPayout.
- trans_ref_num is the transaction reference number that uniquely defines each payout transaction (e.g. 20211270000002); it identifies which payout to cancel. (The sample curl leaves it as an empty string as a placeholder.)
- In a successful response, request_time, actid and tran_status are present; tran_status is a numeric status code (e.g. "88").
- Failure case observed: attempting to cancel an already-canceled payout returns stat=Not_Ok with emsg 'Error Occurred : -103 <ref> is Already Canceled'. A session-key problem returns 'Session Expired : Invalid Session Key'.
